title: wire lt issues to SQLite cache (--live flag to bypass)
description:
Change issues/list.rs run() to use cache-first read path:

1. Open db connection
2. If db exists and last_synced_at is present and age < TTL (default 5 min):
   - Call query_issues(conn, args) -> serve result instantly
   - Print '(cached)' note or staleness in footer
3. If cache is empty: run sync full first, then query
4. If stale (> TTL): serve stale cache immediately, trigger delta sync in background, re-print result after sync completes (or just note staleness)
5. Add --live flag to IssueArgs: bypass cache entirely, hit API directly

TTL should be configurable or at least a named constant.

title: lt search: CLI full-text search command
description:
Add 'lt search <query>' CLI command that searches the local SQLite FTS5 index.

Usage:
  lt search "oauth token"        # phrase search
  lt search "auth*"               # prefix search
  lt search "login bug"           # AND of terms (FTS5 default)

Output: same table format as lt issues (reuse print_table()).

Flags:
  --limit N     (default: 20)
  --live        bypass local index, use Linear API search (future: Linear has a search field in GraphQL)

If FTS index is empty or stale: print a note suggesting 'lt sync full' and fall back to LIKE query on title.

Error if no cache exists at all: 'Run lt sync to build local index first.'

title: Rewrite build.rs code generation with quote+prettyplease
description:
Replace all push_str/format! string concatenation in build.rs with structured quote! macro invocations. This is the main codegen overhaul from ADR Decision 1.

Scope:
- Add build-dependencies to Cargo.toml: quote 1, proc-macro2 1, syn 2 (full+parsing), prettyplease 0.2
- Rewrite gen_stem_key_enum() to return TokenStream using quote!
- Rewrite gen_stem_kind_enum() to return TokenStream using quote!
- Rewrite gen_from_ast() to return TokenStream using quote!
- Rewrite gen_parser_fn() to return TokenStream using quote! (most complex -- Chumsky boilerplate with interpolated match arms)
- Pipe final output through syn::parse2 + prettyplease::unparse before fs::write
- Verify generated search_stems.rs is functionally equivalent (cargo test must pass)

Migration order within this task: gen_stem_key_enum (simplest) -> gen_stem_kind_enum -> gen_from_ast -> gen_parser_fn (hardest).

Keep all existing validation logic (GraphQL schema validation, rerun-if-changed directives) unchanged.

Reference: docs/design/search-codegen-and-filter-expansion-adr.md Decision 1

title: local-first: SQLite cache + delta sync
description: Epic tracking all work to add a local SQLite cache with delta sync to lt. Goal: instant reads from local store, delta sync via updatedAt filter, offline-capable TUI with background refresh.
close_reason: All blocking sub-tasks (bd-25j, bd-103) are closed. Epic complete.

title: Add project, cycle, and creator filter stems
description:
Add the three Tier 1 filter stems identified in the ADR: project, cycle, creator. These are high-value, straightforward entity filters with the same key:value pattern as existing stems.

New TOML entries (build/search_filter_fields.toml):

  [[field]]
  key       = "project"
  gql_field = "project"
  gql_type  = "NullableProjectFilter"

  [[field]]
  key       = "cycle"
  gql_field = "cycle"
  gql_type  = "NullableCycleFilter"

  [[field]]
  key       = "creator"
  gql_field = "creator"
  gql_type  = "NullableUserFilter"

Scope:
- Add the three entries above to search_filter_fields.toml
- Verify build.rs schema validation passes (gql_field + gql_type must match IssueFilter in the GraphQL schema)
- Add SQL WHERE clauses in run_query() in src/tui/search_query.rs for each new stem:
  - project: match against project_name column (LIKE, case-insensitive)
  - cycle: match against cycle_name column (LIKE, case-insensitive)
  - creator: match against creator_name column (LIKE, case-insensitive)
- Check that the local SQLite issues table has the needed columns (project_name, cycle_name, creator_name). If not, add them to the sync/schema code.
- Add the new stems to STEM_KEY_STRINGS in search_query.rs so tab completion picks them up
- Add ParsedQuery fields for the three new stems
- Verify cargo build and cargo test pass

Reference: docs/design/search-codegen-and-filter-expansion-adr.md Decision 3

title: <esc> should trigger resetting state eg. the search terms. use ctrl+c to "clear" instead
Design: parser/AST/grammar for TUI search syntax with tab-completion
description:
Research and design a more sophisticated approach to the TUI search bar query language.

## Motivation

The current implementation in src/tui/search_query.rs uses ad-hoc whitespace splitting and string matching to parse structured query stems (sort:, assignee:, priority:, state:, team:). This works but is fragile and hard to extend.

## Goals

1. **Parser / AST / Grammar**
   Replace the current split-and-match approach with a proper parser that produces an AST. Consider:
   - PEG grammar (e.g. peg or pest crate)
   - Hand-rolled recursive descent
   - nom combinator parser
   - Decide what the AST nodes should look like (Stem, FtsWord, SortExpr, etc.)
   - Error recovery: partial/invalid input should not crash, just produce best-effort results

2. **Token-aware cursor navigation**
   In the TUI search bar, Tab (or Ctrl+Right / similar) should jump between tokens/terms in the query string. This requires the parser to also track byte/char spans for each token so the cursor can land on token boundaries.

3. **Stem auto-complete**
   When the cursor is at a partially-typed stem, Tab should cycle through or pop up completions for known stems: sort:, assignee:, priority:, state:, team:.

4. **Stem-value auto-complete (follow-up)**
   After the stem colon, offer value completions:
   - priority: -> urgent / high / normal / low / none
   - state: -> values pulled live from the DB
   - assignee: -> values pulled live from the DB (or at least 'me')
   - sort: -> updated / created / priority / title / assignee / state / team, with +/- suffix

## Research Questions

- Which parser approach best fits the incremental / always-valid constraint (input is live while user types)?
- How should the AST represent incomplete tokens (user mid-type)?
- What completion UX pattern fits the existing ratatui widget stack best (inline suffix hint vs popup list)?
- Should completion state live in SearchOverlay or a new Completer struct?

## Out of Scope (for this design bead)

- Implementation: this bead produces a design doc / ADR, not code.
- Grammar extensions beyond the current set of stems.

title: help modal should not allow "q" to close the modal, only <esc>
db: upsert_issues() and query_issues()
description:
In src/db/mod.rs (or src/db/issues.rs), add:

upsert_issues(conn, issues: &[Issue]) -> Result<()>
  - INSERT OR REPLACE INTO issues (...) for each issue
  - Sets synced_at to current UTC timestamp

query_issues(conn, filter: &IssueArgs) -> Result<Vec<Issue>>
  - Builds SQL WHERE clause from IssueArgs fields (team, assignee, state, priority, title substring, date ranges)
  - Applies ORDER BY from sort args
  - Returns Vec<Issue>

get_meta(conn, key) and set_meta(conn, key, value) helpers for sync_meta table.
close_reason: implemented and merged into default workspace

title: impl: AST types and parse_query_ast for search query parser
description:
Implement the AST data types and hand-rolled recursive descent parser described in search-query-parser-adr.md (Decisions 1-3).

Deliverables (all in src/tui/search_query.rs):

1. Span struct: pub struct Span { pub start: usize, pub end: usize }

2. StemKey enum: Sort, Assignee, Priority, State, Team

3. StemKind enum: Sort { field: SortField, dir: SortDir }, Assignee { value: String }, Priority { value: String }, State { value: String }, Team { value: String }

4. Token enum: Stem { span, key_span, val_span, kind }, PartialStem { span, key_span, val_span, known_key: Option<StemKey> }, Word { span, text }, Unknown { span, raw }

5. QueryAst struct: pub struct QueryAst { pub raw: String, pub tokens: Vec<Token> }

6. pub fn parse_query_ast(raw: &str) -> QueryAst - single-pass byte scanner (see ADR Decision 3 pseudocode). classify_token logic: if colon present, split at first colon; check key against known StemKey list; if key known and value valid -> Token::Stem; if key known but value invalid/empty -> Token::PartialStem { known_key: Some(...) }; if key unknown -> Token::PartialStem { known_key: None }; if no colon -> Token::Word.

7. impl From<&QueryAst> for ParsedQuery - derives the SQL-ready ParsedQuery from the AST, replacing the current parse_query() free function. ParsedQuery::from(&ast) should produce identical results to parse_query(ast.raw.trim()) for all valid inputs.

Invariants (per ADR):
- Every non-whitespace byte in raw is covered by exactly one token.
- All span fields contain valid UTF-8 char boundaries.
- The parser never panics; any input yields a valid QueryAst.

Tests: add unit tests for parse_query_ast covering span correctness, empty input, partial stems, unknown keys, and From<&QueryAst> parity with parse_query.

Note: keep the existing parse_query() function in place for now; it will be removed when SearchOverlay is wired to use the AST path.
close_reason: AST types, parse_query_ast, and From<&QueryAst> for ParsedQuery implemented with 16 new unit tests, all passing

title: impl: render inline suffix hint in append_text_input_spans
description:
Render the inline ghost-text suffix hint from Completer::hint_suffix() in the search bar UI (Decision 5, Completion UX: Inline Suffix Hint, in the ADR).

Deliverables (src/tui/ui.rs):

1. Find the function append_text_input_spans (or the equivalent that renders the search bar TextInput spans in Mode::Search).

2. After the existing cursor span, append an additional Span with:
   - Text: completer.hint_suffix() if Some, otherwise skip.
   - Style: dim/dark-grey (ratatui Style::default().fg(Color::DarkGray) or similar).

3. The hint must only appear when the TUI is in Mode::Search and completer.hint_suffix() returns Some.

4. The hint span must not affect cursor position calculations -- it is purely decorative.

The Completer reference should be read from SearchOverlay::completer. The ui.rs render function already receives the App/SearchOverlay state; pass it through (or through a reference) as needed.

Dependency: this bead depends on bd-3qb (Completer must be wired into SearchOverlay first).
close_reason: Ghost-text hint span rendered after cursor in search bar using completer.hint_suffix(), styled DarkGray

title: the header does not update to reflect the sort: term. it just says "sort:updated-" forever. similarly the table header UPDATED does not update to +
tui: background delta sync thread + synced-at footer
description:
In the TUI, add a background sync thread so the UI starts instantly from cache and refreshes silently.

- On TUI startup: query SQLite immediately, render issue list
- Spawn a background std::thread that runs delta sync
- Thread communicates back to TUI via a std::sync::mpsc channel (SyncEvent::Done(Vec<Issue>) or SyncEvent::Error(String))
- TUI polls the channel in its event loop (non-blocking try_recv)
- On SyncEvent::Done: update issue list and re-render
- Footer bar shows: 'synced X min ago' (read from sync_meta) or 'syncing...' while background thread is active
- If no cache exists yet: show 'Syncing...' overlay until first sync completes

This delivers the core local-first UX: zero-latency startup, silent background refresh.

Show a brief 'Loading...' state while the request is in flight.

title: tui: vim-style half/full page scroll
description:
Add Ctrl+D (half page down), Ctrl+U (half page up), Ctrl+F / PageDown (full page down), Ctrl+B / PageUp (full page up).

Store viewport_height: u16 in App. Update it in ui::render_table() each frame: viewport_height = table_area.height.saturating_sub(1) (subtract table header row).

In event handler:
  Ctrl+D -> move_by(+(viewport_height/2))
  Ctrl+U -> move_by(-(viewport_height/2))  (saturating)
  Ctrl+F / PageDown -> move_by(+viewport_height)
  Ctrl+B / PageUp   -> move_by(-viewport_height)

move_by(delta: i32): clamp new index to 0..issues.len()-1, call table_state.select(Some(new_i)).
close_reason: Ctrl+d/u half-page, Ctrl+f/b + PageDown/Up full-page. viewport_height set in render_table each frame. move_by(delta) with i32 clamp.

title: sync delta: fetch updatedAt > last_synced_at, upsert
description:
Implement 'lt sync' (default sync command) for incremental updates.

- Read last_synced_at from sync_meta
- If missing, delegate to 'sync full'
- Otherwise: add filter { updatedAt: { gt: last_synced_at } } to GraphQL query
- Paginate through results (usually 0-1 pages for recent delta)
- Upsert into SQLite
- Update last_synced_at

NOTE: Linear does not expose deleted issues via this filter. Deletions are not handled yet (future work).
NOTE: issues that are archived/completed may not appear in default query - consider filter for all states.

title: tui: / search overlay using FTS5
description:
In the TUI, pressing '/' opens an incremental search overlay.

Behavior:
  - A search bar appears at the bottom (above footer)
  - User types query; issue list filters in real-time as they type (debounce ~150ms)
  - Uses fts_search() against local SQLite FTS5 index -- instant, no API call
  - Results replace the main list view while search is active
  - Esc clears search and returns to full list
  - Enter confirms and leaves search mode with filtered results visible
  - Selected issue in search results supports all normal keybindings (Enter for detail, o for browser, etc.)

If FTS index not populated: show 'Search unavailable: run lt sync first' in the search bar.

title: tui: issue detail pane (Enter to expand, Esc to close)
description:
In the TUI, pressing Enter on a selected issue opens a detail view.

Layout options (pick one during impl):
  - Full-screen overlay replacing the list
  - Vertical split: list on left (~40%), detail on right (~60%)

Detail pane shows:
  - Header: IDENTIFIER - Title (state, priority, assignee, team)
  - Body: description rendered as plain text (strip markdown syntax minimally: **bold** -> bold, etc.)
  - Comments section: each comment with author + date + body
  - Scroll with j/k inside the pane

Keybindings:
  Enter  - open detail pane
  Esc / q - close pane, return to list
  o      - open in browser (works in both list and detail view)

Start without caching descriptions -- fetch on demand when Enter is pressed. Show a brief 'Loading...' while fetching.

title: issues: SQL filter layer from IssueArgs
description:
In src/db/issues.rs (or similar), implement build_sql_filter(args: &IssueArgs) that produces a SQL WHERE clause + bind params mirroring what build_filter() does for GraphQL.

Filter mappings:
  --team        -> team_name LIKE ? (case-insensitive) or team_key = ?
  --assignee me -> assignee_name = (viewer name from auth)
  --assignee X  -> assignee_name LIKE ?
  --no-assignee -> assignee_name IS NULL
  --state       -> state_name LIKE ?
  --priority    -> priority_label = ?
  --title       -> title LIKE ? (substring)
  --created-after/before  -> created_at >= / <=
  --updated-after/before  -> updated_at >= / <=

Also wire sort args to ORDER BY clause (mirrors build_sort()).
close_reason: implemented and merged into default workspace

title: Generate SortField enum and sort: stem from IssueSortInput schema type
description:
The SortField enum (src/issues/mod.rs) and the sort: stem in the parser are currently hard-coded with 7 variants: Updated, Created, Priority, Title, Assignee, State, Team. The Linear GraphQL schema defines IssueSortInput with 22 fields:

  accumulatedStateUpdatedAt, assignee, completedAt, createdAt, customer,
  customerCount, customerImportantCount, customerRevenue, cycle, delegate,
  dueDate, estimate, label, labelGroup, linkCount, manual, milestone,
  priority, project, rootIssue, slaStatus, team, title, updatedAt,
  workflowState

The sort: stem should be generated the same way filter stems are: validated against the schema at build time, with variants and match arms produced by build.rs (using quote after bd-117 lands).

Hard-coded sort artifacts to replace:

1. SortField enum (src/issues/mod.rs:10-19) -- 7 variants + label(), next(), prev() impls
2. parse_sort_value() (src/tui/search_query.rs:268-287) -- match on sort value strings
3. sort_col() (src/tui/search_query.rs:313-323) -- map SortField -> SQLite column name
4. build_sort() (src/issues/sort.rs) -- map SortField -> IssueSortInput JSON key
5. The StemKind::Sort hard-coded arm in gen_parser_fn() (build.rs)

Approach:
- Add a [[sort_field]] table to search_filter_fields.toml (or a new TOML file)
- Each entry: key (user-typed), gql_field (IssueSortInput field name, schema-validated), sql_col (SQLite ORDER BY column)
- build.rs validates entries against IssueSortInput in the GraphQL schema (same pattern as IssueFilter validation)
- build.rs generates: SortField enum, parse_sort_value(), sort_col(), build_sort()
- Curate a practical subset of IssueSortInput fields (not all 22 are useful locally)

This bead depends on the codegen overhaul (bd-117) and the epic (bd-zqo) completing first.

Reference: docs/design/search-codegen-and-filter-expansion-adr.md

title: lt inbox --limit: cap total results, not just page size
description: Currently --limit N is passed as the per-page 'first' GraphQL argument, so it only caps a single page. For precise total-count control across multiple pages, fetch_notifications needs a separate max_total ceiling. Refactor fetch_notifications to accept max_total: Option<usize> and stop paginating once that many items have been collected. Wire --limit through as max_total.

title: add rusqlite dep + src/db/ scaffold (schema, migrations)
description:
Add rusqlite (bundled feature) to Cargo.toml. Create src/db/mod.rs with:
- open_db() -> returns Connection, creates ~/.local/share/lt/lt.db
- run_migrations() on open: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS issues (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, identifier TEXT NOT NULL, title TEXT NOT NULL, priority_label TEXT NOT NULL, state_name TEXT NOT NULL, assignee_name TEXT, team_name TEXT NOT NULL, team_key TEXT, created_at TEXT NOT NULL, updated_at TEXT NOT NULL, synced_at TEXT NOT NULL)

This is the foundation for all other local-first work.

title: using "s" in the tui gets an error from the Linear api
impl: Completer struct for stem-key tab completion
description:
Implement the Completer struct and its methods described in search-query-parser-adr.md (Decision 4).

Deliverables (all in src/tui/search_query.rs, or a new src/tui/completer.rs if preferable):

1. CompletionContext enum:
   - StemKey { prefix: String }
   - StemValue { key: StemKey, prefix: String }  (stub only; Phase 2)
   - Word
   - Gap

2. Completer struct:
   pub struct Completer {
       pub active_token: Option<Token>,
       pub context: CompletionContext,
       pub candidates: Vec<String>,
       pub selected: usize,
       pub candidates_pending: bool,  // Phase 2 placeholder
   }

3. impl Completer:
   - fn new() -> Self - initialise with Gap context, empty candidates
   - pub fn update(&mut self, ast: &QueryAst, cursor: usize) - recompute active_token, context, candidates, reset selected to 0. Logic:
     * Find which token (if any) the cursor byte offset falls inside (token.span.start <= cursor <= token.span.end).
     * If inside a PartialStem with colon present and cursor <= key_span.end: context = StemKey { prefix = key text up to cursor }.
     * If inside a Word or PartialStem with key unknown: context = Word.
     * If cursor in whitespace / past end: context = Gap.
     * Candidates for StemKey: filter [sort:, assignee:, priority:, state:, team:] by prefix (case-insensitive).
     * Candidates for Word / Gap: empty list (token boundary jumping uses no candidates).
   - pub fn hint_suffix(&self) -> Option<&str> - returns untyped suffix of candidates[selected] relative to the already-typed prefix (for inline ghost-text rendering). Returns None if no candidates or candidates[selected] does not start with prefix.
   - pub fn apply_tab(&mut self, input: &mut TextInput, ast: &QueryAst, forward: bool) - apply one Tab press:
     * If context is StemKey and candidates non-empty: cycle selected (+1 or -1, wrapping); replace key portion of input (key_span.start..cursor) with candidates[selected]; move cursor just after the inserted colon.
     * Otherwise (Word / Gap / empty candidates): jump cursor to start of next token boundary (forward=true) or start of prev token boundary (forward=false). Wrap around if no next/prev token.

Dependency: this bead depends on bd-22c (AST types must exist first).

Tests: unit tests for Completer::update (StemKey context with prefix, Gap context, Word context) and hint_suffix.
close_reason: Completer struct with CompletionContext, update, hint_suffix, apply_tab implemented; StemValue stubbed for Phase 2; 13 new unit tests, all 60 passing

title: tui: write op keybindings with optimistic SQLite update
description:
Wire write mutations into the TUI with optimistic local updates.

Keybindings (on selected issue in list or detail pane):
  s  - cycle state: show a small popup list of workflow states for this issue's team, select with j/k + Enter
  p  - cycle priority: popup with none/urgent/high/normal/low choices
  a  - reassign: popup with team member list (fetch once, cache in memory)

Optimistic update flow:
  1. User selects new value in popup
  2. Update SQLite immediately (user sees change instantly)
  3. Fire mutation in background thread
  4. On success: confirm (no-op if SQLite already correct)
  5. On error: revert SQLite, show error in footer

The popup widget should be reusable for all three operations (generic list-picker popup).

title: Remove dead TOML metadata from search filter allowlist
description:
Strip the unused sql_col, sql_op, sql_lower, and value_hint fields from build/search_filter_fields.toml and the corresponding FieldSpec struct in build.rs. These fields are deserialized but never consumed by code generation (all annotated #[allow(dead_code)]). The actual SQL logic lives hand-written in run_query() in src/tui/search_query.rs.

Scope:
- Remove sql_col, sql_op, sql_lower, value_hint from each [[field]] entry in build/search_filter_fields.toml
- Remove those fields (and their #[allow(dead_code)] annotations) from the FieldSpec struct in build.rs
- Add a brief comment in run_query() documenting the per-field SQL mapping (the knowledge that was in the TOML)
- Verify cargo build and cargo test pass

The TOML should be reduced to just: key, gql_field, gql_type per entry.

--- Future context (not in scope) ---

The TOML metadata was intended to drive SQL generation but fell short because 3 of 5 fields need special handling the metadata cannot express:
- assignee: special 'me' branch (LIKE vs literal =)
- priority: normalise_priority() value transform, skip-if-None
- team: OR across two columns (team_name, team_key)
- label: TOML says 'label_names' but actual column is 'labels'

Only 'state' is a clean match. A future iteration could introduce a richer sql_match schema (multi-column OR, value transforms, COALESCE, bind patterns) and generate run_query() from the TOML. This becomes worthwhile at ~15+ filter stems. The quote rewrite (bd-117) is a prerequisite for that since generating SQL-building Rust via push_str would be even worse. See ADR for the proposed schema shape.

Reference: docs/design/search-codegen-and-filter-expansion-adr.md Decision 2

title: Add build.rs with graphql-parser and search_filter_fields.toml allowlist
description:
Implement the build.rs code generation foundation for the v2 search parser (see docs/design/search-parser-v2-adr.md).

Tasks:
- Add graphql-parser to [build-dependencies] in Cargo.toml
- Create build/search_filter_fields.toml with allowlist entries for: assignee, priority, state, team, label (and any other agreed initial set)
- Write build.rs to: parse docs/reference/linear-schema-definition.graphql, extract IssueFilter fields, validate each allowlist entry (field name + gql_type) against the parsed schema, fail the build loudly if a field is missing or the type mismatches
- The build script output (search_stems.rs) is generated in the next bead; this bead just needs the validation pipeline working and the TOML config finalized
close_reason: build.rs validation pipeline and search_filter_fields.toml allowlist implemented and verified compiling

title: Optimize error-span lookup in search query highlight rendering
description: The error_segments helper in src/tui/ui.rs checks error-span membership with a linear scan over all error spans per character. For typical short query strings this is negligible, but for correctness and future-proofing the scan could be replaced with a sorted-interval approach (sort spans by start, binary-search for the first candidate, then walk forward). Deferred from bd-2gj.

title: impl: wire QueryAst and Completer into SearchOverlay
description:
Wire the new AST and Completer into SearchOverlay (Decision 6 of the ADR).

Deliverables (src/tui/mod.rs):

1. Add fields to SearchOverlay:
   pub ast: search_query::QueryAst,
   pub completer: search_query::Completer,

2. Update SearchOverlay::new() to initialise both fields (parse_query_ast of the default query string).

3. Update SearchOverlay::run_search() to:
   - Call parse_query_ast(raw) and store result in self.ast.
   - Derive ParsedQuery via ParsedQuery::from(&self.ast) instead of calling parse_query() directly.
   - Call self.completer.update(&self.ast, self.query.cursor) after every search.

4. Handle Tab / Shift-Tab in SearchOverlay's key handler (wherever on_key / handle_key is called for Mode::Search in the event loop):
   - On KeyCode::Tab (no modifiers): call self.completer.apply_tab(&mut self.query, &self.ast, true); re-parse and re-search.
   - On KeyCode::BackTab (Shift-Tab): call self.completer.apply_tab(&mut self.query, &self.ast, false); re-parse and re-search.
   - Tab / Shift-Tab must NOT be forwarded to TextInput::handle_key.

Dependency: this bead depends on bd-35l (Completer must exist first).
close_reason: QueryAst and Completer wired into SearchOverlay; Tab/BackTab dispatch added; ParsedQuery now derived via From<&QueryAst>

title: lt inbox --all --limit=N does not work
FINDING: client-api.linear.app rejects OAuth tokens
description:
Empirically verified 2026-02-25 via lt sync probe.

GET https://client-api.linear.app/sync/bootstrap?type=full&onlyModels=Issue
Authorization: Bearer <oauth_token>

Response: HTTP 403
{
  "error": "OAuth token authentication not allowed on this endpoint",
  "extensions": {
    "type": "forbidden",
    "code": "FORBIDDEN",
    "statusCode": 403,
    "userPresentableMessage": "OAuth token authentication not allowed on this endpoint"
  }
}

Conclusion: Linear explicitly gates client-api.linear.app to browser session auth.
OAuth tokens (including those from the documented public OAuth2 flow) are refused.
The "local-first via Linear sync protocol" approach is not viable.

Alternatives:
  1. Public GraphQL + periodic polling + local SQLite cache (recommended)
  2. Webhooks (requires reachable server, not suitable for a TUI)
  3. Personal API tokens -- likely same result since the check is on auth METHOD
     not auth credential type. Could be tested but low probability of success.
close_reason: Finding documented. Pivot to GraphQL polling + local cache.

db: FTS5 virtual table for issue title + description
description:
Extend the SQLite schema with a FTS5 full-text search virtual table.

Schema addition (run in migrations):
  CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS issues_fts USING fts5(
    identifier,
    title,
    description,
    content='issues',
    content_rowid='rowid'
  );

Keep FTS index in sync with issues table:
  - After upsert_issues(), also INSERT/REPLACE into issues_fts
  - Or use FTS5 content table feature with triggers

Search function:
  fts_search(conn, query: &str, limit: usize) -> Result<Vec<Issue>>
  - SELECT issues.* FROM issues JOIN issues_fts ON issues.rowid = issues_fts.rowid WHERE issues_fts MATCH ?
  - Supports FTS5 prefix queries (e.g. 'oauth*'), phrase queries ('oauth token')

Note: description field requires storing descriptions in the issues table (add description column) and populating it during sync.

title: Entering search mode triggers full table re-render causing lag
description: When pressing '/' to enter search mode, the entire table immediately re-renders before any input is given. This causes noticeable lag/slowness on mode transition. Search mode entry should be instant and only re-render when the user has actually typed a query.

comments.author: claude-code-2.1.56
comments.text: Group B (search logic): touches src/tui/mod.rs ~line 2384 where search_overlay is initialized. Entering search mode calls run_search() immediately even with empty query; the empty-query early return should prevent re-render but the overlay creation itself may trigger a re-render.

title: Epic: search codegen overhaul and filter expansion
description:
Implement the three decisions from docs/design/search-codegen-and-filter-expansion-adr.md:

1. Replace push_str code generation in build.rs with quote+prettyplease
2. Remove dead TOML metadata (sql_col, sql_op, sql_lower, value_hint)
3. Add project, cycle, creator filter stems

Children are structured for maximal agent concurrency -- all three tasks can run in parallel.
